paths.artha_niti: Path of Wealth & Strategy — for career, money, financial planning
paths.raja_niti: Path of Leadership — for management, politics, decision-making
paths.vyavahara_niti: Path of Conduct — for relationships, social behavior, trust
paths.vidya_niti: Path of Education — for learning, knowledge, self-improvement

slokas.sanskrit: दुर्जनस्य च सर्पस्य वरं सर्पो न दुर्जनः। सर्पो दशति कालेन दुर्जनस्तु पदे पदे॥
slokas.transliteration: durjanasya ca sarpasya varaṁ sarpo na durjanaḥ | sarpo daśati kālena durjanas tu pade pade ||
slokas.english_meaning: Between a snake and a wicked person, the snake is better. A snake bites only occasionally, but a wicked person harms at every step.
slokas.when_to_use: When dealing with someone consistently harmful or manipulative. Recognize toxicity and maintain distance.

slokas.sanskrit: पुस्तकेषु च या विद्या परहस्तेषु च यद्धनम्। उपयोगे तु या विद्या तद्धनं च न विद्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: pustakeṣu ca yā vidyā para-hasteṣu ca yad dhanam | upayoge tu yā vidyā tad dhanaṁ ca na vidyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Knowledge that remains in books and wealth that is in another's hands — when the time of need arrives, neither is truly yours.
slokas.when_to_use: When relying too much on theory without practice, or depending on others for financial security.

slokas.sanskrit: एकोऽपि गुणवान् पुत्रो निर्गुणैश्च शतैर्वरः। एकश्चन्द्रस्तमो हन्ति न च ताराः सहस्रशः॥
slokas.transliteration: eko 'pi guṇavān putro nirguṇaiś ca śatair varaḥ | ekaś candras tamo hanti na ca tārāḥ sahasraśaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: One virtuous child is better than a hundred without virtue. A single moon dispels darkness, not thousands of stars.
slokas.when_to_use: When chasing quantity over quality — in work, relationships, or parenting. Focus on excellence, not numbers.

slokas.sanskrit: विद्या मित्रं प्रवासेषु भार्या मित्रं गृहेषु च। व्याधितस्यौषधं मित्रं धर्मो मित्रं मृतस्य च॥
slokas.transliteration: vidyā mitraṁ pravāseṣu bhāryā mitraṁ gṛheṣu ca | vyādhitasyauṣadhaṁ mitraṁ dharmo mitraṁ mṛtasya ca ||
slokas.english_meaning: Education is your friend in foreign lands, a good spouse is your friend at home, medicine is the friend of the sick, and righteousness is the friend of the dead.
slokas.when_to_use: When prioritizing what truly matters in life. Invest in education, relationships, health, and good deeds.

slokas.sanskrit: आत्मनः प्रतिकूलानि परेषां न समाचरेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: ātmanaḥ pratikūlāni pareṣāṁ na samācaret ||
slokas.english_meaning: Do not do unto others what is unpleasant to yourself.
slokas.when_to_use: When tempted to treat someone poorly. The simplest rule for all human interaction.

slokas.sanskrit: अर्थनाशं मनस्तापं गृहे दुश्चरितानि च। वञ्चनं चापमानं च मतिमान्न प्रकाशयेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: arthanāśaṁ manastāpaṁ gṛhe duścaritāni ca | vañcanaṁ cāpamānaṁ ca matimān na prakāśayet ||
slokas.english_meaning: A wise person does not reveal their financial losses, mental anguish, family scandals, experiences of being cheated, or humiliations suffered.
slokas.when_to_use: When tempted to overshare personal failures or losses publicly. Protect your dignity and privacy strategically.

slokas.sanskrit: अतिरूपेण वा सीता अतिगर्वेण रावणः। अतिदानाद्बलिर्बद्धो ह्यतिसर्वत्र वर्जयेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: atirūpeṇa vā sītā atigarbeṇa rāvaṇaḥ | atidānād balir baddho hy ati-sarvatra varjayet ||
slokas.english_meaning: Excess of beauty caused Sita's suffering, excess of pride destroyed Ravana, excess of generosity bound King Bali. Excess of anything should be avoided.
slokas.when_to_use: When going overboard in any area of life. Even good qualities become harmful in excess.

slokas.sanskrit: माता शत्रुः पिता वैरी येन बालो न पाठितः। न शोभते सभामध्ये हंसमध्ये बको यथा॥
slokas.transliteration: mātā śatruḥ pitā vairī yena bālo na pāṭhitaḥ | na śobhate sabhā-madhye haṁsa-madhye bako yathā ||
slokas.english_meaning: The mother is an enemy and the father an adversary who do not educate their child. An uneducated person in an assembly of the learned is like a crane among swans.
slokas.when_to_use: When questioning the importance of investing in children's education. Education is the greatest gift parents can give.

slokas.sanskrit: सेवितव्यो महावृक्षः फलच्छायासमन्वितः। यदि दैवात्फलं नास्ति छाया केन निवार्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: sevitavyo mahā-vṛkṣaḥ phala-cchāyā-samanvitaḥ | yadi daivāt phalaṁ nāsti chāyā kena nivāryate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Take shelter of a great tree that provides fruit and shade. Even if by fate it bears no fruit, who can deny its shade?
slokas.when_to_use: When choosing whom to associate with professionally. Align with worthy people — even if one benefit fails, others remain.

slokas.sanskrit: यस्य नास्ति स्वयं प्रज्ञा शास्त्रं तस्य करोति किम्। लोचनाभ्यां विहीनस्य दर्पणः किं करिष्यति॥
slokas.transliteration: yasya nāsti svayaṁ prajñā śāstraṁ tasya karoti kim | locanābhyāṁ vihīnasya darpaṇaḥ kiṁ kariṣyati ||
slokas.english_meaning: Of what use are scriptures to one who has no intelligence of their own? What can a mirror do for one who has no eyes?
slokas.when_to_use: When blindly following advice without applying your own judgment. Books and teachers can only help if you think for yourself.

slokas.sanskrit: उपार्जितानां वित्तानां त्याग एव हि रक्षणम्। तडागोदरसंस्थानां परिवाह इवाम्भसाम्॥
slokas.transliteration: upārjitānāṁ vittānāṁ tyāga eva hi rakṣaṇam | taḍāgodara-saṁsthānāṁ parivāha ivāmbhasām ||
slokas.english_meaning: The way to protect earned wealth is through wise spending and giving, just as stagnant water in a lake is preserved by letting it flow.
slokas.when_to_use: When hoarding money out of fear. Wealth grows through wise circulation — invest, give, and keep it flowing.

slokas.sanskrit: कार्ये निमित्ते प्रकृतौ गतायां प्रत्यर्पणं चापि सतां स्वभावः। आभ्यन्तरे चूर्णतिले करालं बाह्ये ददाति त्विषमिक्षुयन्त्रम्॥
slokas.transliteration: kārye nimitte prakṛtau gatāyāṁ pratyarpaṇaṁ cāpi satāṁ svabhāvaḥ | ābhyantare cūrṇa-tile karālaṁ bāhye dadāti tviṣam ikṣu-yantram ||
slokas.english_meaning: Watch how the sugarcane press crushes everything inside but yields sweet juice on the outside. Similarly, judge people by their hidden nature, not their outward sweetness.
slokas.when_to_use: When someone appears overly sweet or generous outwardly. Look beneath the surface before trusting.

slokas.sanskrit: सत्यं माता पिता ज्ञानं धर्मो भ्राता दया सखा। शान्तिः पत्नी क्षमा पुत्रः षडेते मम बान्धवाः॥
slokas.transliteration: satyaṁ mātā pitā jñānaṁ dharmo bhrātā dayā sakhā | śāntiḥ patnī kṣamā putraḥ ṣaḍ ete mama bāndhavāḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Truth is my mother, knowledge my father, righteousness my brother, compassion my friend, peace my wife, and forgiveness my son — these six are my true relatives.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling alone or unsupported. Build your inner family with virtues that never abandon you.

slokas.sanskrit: अनागतविधाता च प्रत्युत्पन्नमतिस्तथा। द्वावेतौ सुखमेधेते यद्भविष्यो विनश्यति॥
slokas.transliteration: anāgata-vidhātā ca pratyutpanna-matis tathā | dvāv etau sukham edhete yad-bhaviṣyo vinaśyati ||
slokas.english_meaning: Two types of people prosper: those who plan for the future and those who think on their feet. The one who relies on fate alone perishes.
slokas.when_to_use: When leaving things to chance instead of planning ahead. Success requires preparation and adaptability.

slokas.sanskrit: दुराचारी दुर्दृष्टिर्दुरावासी च दुर्जनः। यन्मैत्री क्रियते पुम्भिर्नरस्तैर्दश्यते ध्रुवम्॥
slokas.transliteration: durācārī durdṛṣṭir durāvāsī ca durjanaḥ | yan maitrī kriyate pumbhir naras tair daśyate dhruvam ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who befriends a person of bad character, bad vision, or bad dwelling is certain to be destroyed. Friendship with the wicked leads to ruin.
slokas.when_to_use: When maintaining friendships that are dragging you down. Your company shapes your destiny.

slokas.sanskrit: अनवस्थितकार्यस्य न जने न वने सुखम्। जने दहति संसर्गो वने सङ्गविवर्जनम्॥
slokas.transliteration: anavasthita-kāryasya na jane na vane sukham | jane dahati saṁsargo vane saṅga-vivarjanam ||
slokas.english_meaning: A person who cannot discipline their mind finds happiness neither in society nor in solitude. In society, company burns them; in the forest, loneliness torments them.
slokas.when_to_use: When constantly unhappy regardless of circumstances. The problem is within — work on inner discipline first.

slokas.sanskrit: परोक्षे कार्यहन्तारं प्रत्यक्षे प्रियवादिनम्। वर्जयेत्तादृशं मित्रं विषकुम्भं पयोमुखम्॥
slokas.transliteration: parokṣe kārya-hantāraṁ pratyakṣe priya-vādinam | varjayet tādṛśaṁ mitraṁ viṣa-kumbhaṁ payo-mukham ||
slokas.english_meaning: Avoid that friend who speaks sweetly to your face but sabotages your work behind your back — like a pot of poison with a surface of milk.
slokas.when_to_use: When suspecting someone is being two-faced. Sweet words and harmful actions are the hallmark of a false friend.

slokas.sanskrit: वरयेत्कुलजां प्राज्ञो विरूपामपि कन्यकाम्। रूपशीलां न नीचस्य विवाहः सदृशे कुले॥
slokas.transliteration: varayet kulajāṁ prājño virūpām api kanyakām | rūpa-śīlāṁ na nīcasya vivāhaḥ sadṛśe kule ||
slokas.english_meaning: A wise person should marry into a family of good character, even if the bride is not beautiful. Marriage should be with equals in values, not based on looks alone.
slokas.when_to_use: When choosing a life partner. Prioritize character, family values, and compatibility over superficial attractions.

slokas.sanskrit: अधमा धनमिच्छन्ति धनं मानं च मध्यमाः। उत्तमाः मानमिच्छन्ति मानो हि महतां धनम्॥
slokas.transliteration: adhamā dhanam icchanti dhanaṁ mānaṁ ca madhyamāḥ | uttamāḥ mānam icchanti māno hi mahatāṁ dhanam ||
slokas.english_meaning: The lowest seek only wealth, the average seek both wealth and respect, but the greatest seek respect alone — for honor is the true wealth of the great.
slokas.when_to_use: When choosing between a lucrative but dishonorable path and one that preserves your integrity. Aim for respect over riches.

slokas.sanskrit: न विश्वसेत्कुमित्रे च मित्रे चापि न विश्वसेत्। कदाचित्कुपितं मित्रं सर्वं गुह्यं प्रकाशयेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: na viśvaset kumitreṣu ca mitre cāpi na viśvaset | kadācit kupitaṁ mitraṁ sarvaṁ guhyaṁ prakāśayet ||
slokas.english_meaning: Do not trust a bad friend, and do not put too much trust even in a good friend. An angry friend may one day reveal all your secrets.
slokas.when_to_use: When tempted to share deep secrets. Guard your private matters carefully — even friends can turn.

slokas.sanskrit: तावन्मौनेन नीयन्ते कोकिलैश्चैव वासराः। यावत्सर्वजनानन्ददायिनी वाक्प्रवर्तते॥
slokas.transliteration: tāvan maunena nīyante kokilaiś caiva vāsarāḥ | yāvat sarva-janānanda-dāyinī vāk pravartate ||
slokas.english_meaning: The cuckoo remains silent until it can sing sweetly and delight everyone. Speak only when your words can bring value and joy.
slokas.when_to_use: When deciding whether to speak up or stay silent. Wait for the right moment — silence is better than premature speech.

slokas.sanskrit: आलस्यं हि मनुष्याणां शरीरस्थो महान् रिपुः। नास्त्युद्यमसमो बन्धुः कृत्वा यं नावसीदति॥
slokas.transliteration: ālasyaṁ hi manuṣyāṇāṁ śarīrastho mahān ripuḥ | nāsty udyama-samo bandhuḥ kṛtvā yaṁ nāvasīdati ||
slokas.english_meaning: Laziness is the greatest enemy dwelling within the human body. There is no friend like hard work — one who works hard never suffers.
slokas.when_to_use: When procrastinating or making excuses. Your biggest enemy is not outside — it is your own laziness.

slokas.sanskrit: उद्योगे नास्ति दारिद्र्यं जपतो नास्ति पातकम्। मौनेन कलहो नास्ति नास्ति जागरिते भयम्॥
slokas.transliteration: udyoge nāsti dāridryaṁ japato nāsti pātakam | maunena kalaho nāsti nāsti jāgarite bhayam ||
slokas.english_meaning: There is no poverty for the industrious, no sin for one who prays, no quarrel for the silent, and no fear for one who stays alert.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling overwhelmed by problems. Four simple remedies: work hard, pray, stay silent in conflict, and remain alert.

slokas.sanskrit: श्रुत्वा धर्मं विजानाति श्रुत्वा त्यजति दुर्मतिम्। श्रुत्वा ज्ञानमवाप्नोति श्रुत्वा मोक्षमवाप्नुयात्॥
slokas.transliteration: śrutvā dharmaṁ vijānāti śrutvā tyajati durmatim | śrutvā jñānam avāpnoti śrutvā mokṣam avāpnuyāt ||
slokas.english_meaning: By listening, one understands righteousness; by listening, one abandons foolishness; by listening, one gains knowledge; by listening, one attains liberation.
slokas.when_to_use: When you talk more than you listen. The greatest learning comes from listening carefully to others.

slokas.sanskrit: शनैः कन्था शनैः पन्था शनैः पर्वतमस्तके। शनैर्विद्या शनैर्वित्तं पञ्चैतानि शनैः शनैः॥
slokas.transliteration: śanaiḥ kanthā śanaiḥ panthā śanaiḥ parvata-mastake | śanair vidyā śanair vittaṁ pañcaitāni śanaiḥ śanaiḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Stitch by stitch a garment is made, step by step a journey is completed, slowly a mountain peak is reached. Knowledge and wealth too come gradually — patience is key.
slokas.when_to_use: When expecting instant results in career, finances, or learning. Great things are built slowly and steadily.

slokas.sanskrit: अपृष्टो कस्यचिद्ब्रूयान्न पृष्टो बहुभाषते। जानन्नपि हि मेधावी जडवल्लोक आचरेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: apṛṣṭo kasyacid brūyān na pṛṣṭo bahu-bhāṣate | jānann api hi medhāvī jaḍaval loka ācaret ||
slokas.english_meaning: Do not offer unsolicited advice, and even when asked, do not talk too much. A truly intelligent person sometimes behaves like a fool in the world.
slokas.when_to_use: When tempted to show off knowledge or give unwanted advice. True wisdom often lies in restraint.

slokas.sanskrit: तद्भोजनं यद्द्विजभुक्तशेषं तत्सौहृदं यत्क्रियते परस्मिन्। सा प्राज्ञता या न करोति पापं प्रो धर्म एको गृहमेधिनां तु॥
slokas.transliteration: tad bhojanaṁ yad dvija-bhukta-śeṣaṁ tat sauhṛdaṁ yat kriyate parasmin | sā prājñatā yā na karoti pāpaṁ pro dharma eko gṛha-medhināṁ tu ||
slokas.english_meaning: True food is what remains after feeding the worthy. True friendship is what is offered selflessly. True wisdom is that which keeps one from sin. For householders, this is the highest dharma.
slokas.when_to_use: When prioritizing self-interest over service. True fulfillment comes from generosity and righteous living.

slokas.sanskrit: परस्परस्य मर्माणि ये भाषन्ते नराधमाः। त एव विलयं यान्ति वल्मीकोदरसर्पवत्॥
slokas.transliteration: parasparasya marmāṇi ye bhāṣante narādhamāḥ | ta eva vilayaṁ yānti valmīkodara-sarpavat ||
slokas.english_meaning: Those lowly people who expose each other's weaknesses and secrets eventually destroy themselves, like snakes within an anthill that consume each other.
slokas.when_to_use: When tempted to gossip or expose someone's weakness. Such behavior ultimately destroys the gossiper too.

slokas.sanskrit: यस्मिन् देशे न सम्मानो न वृत्तिर्न च बान्धवाः। न च विद्यागमोऽप्यस्ति वासस्तत्र न कारयेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: yasmin deśe na sammāno na vṛttir na ca bāndhavāḥ | na ca vidyāgamo 'py asti vāsas tatra na kārayet ||
slokas.english_meaning: Do not live in a place where there is no respect, no livelihood, no relatives or friends, and no opportunity for learning.
slokas.when_to_use: When deciding whether to stay or leave a city, job, or environment. These four factors determine if a place is right for you.

slokas.sanskrit: विपदि धैर्यमथाभ्युदये क्षमा सदसि वाक्पटुता युधि विक्रमः। यशसि चाभिरुचिर्व्यसनं श्रुतौ प्रकृतिसिद्धमिदं हि महात्मनाम्॥
slokas.transliteration: vipadi dhairyam athābhyudaye kṣamā sadasi vāk-paṭutā yudhi vikramaḥ | yaśasi cābhirucir vyasanaṁ śrutau prakṛti-siddham idaṁ hi mahātmanām ||
slokas.english_meaning: Courage in adversity, humility in prosperity, eloquence in assembly, valor in battle, love of glory, and devotion to learning — these are the natural qualities of great souls.
slokas.when_to_use: When aspiring to true leadership or greatness. These six qualities define a person of noble character.

slokas.sanskrit: त्यजेदेकं कुलस्यार्थे ग्रामस्यार्थे कुलं त्यजेत्। ग्रामं जनपदस्यार्थे आत्मार्थे पृथिवीं त्यजेत्॥
slokas.transliteration: tyajed ekaṁ kulasyārthe grāmasyārthe kulaṁ tyajet | grāmaṁ janapadasyārthe ātmārthe pṛthivīṁ tyajet ||
slokas.english_meaning: Sacrifice one person for the family, one family for the village, one village for the nation, and the whole world for the sake of the self (soul).
slokas.when_to_use: When facing tough decisions that require sacrifice. Understand the hierarchy of values and act accordingly.

slokas.sanskrit: सन्तोषामृततृप्तानां यत्सुखं शान्तिरेव च। न च तद्धनलुब्धानामितश्चेतश्च धावताम्॥
slokas.transliteration: santoṣāmṛta-tṛptānāṁ yat sukhaṁ śāntir eva ca | na ca tad dhana-lubdhānām itaś cetaś ca dhāvatām ||
slokas.english_meaning: The happiness and peace that come to those satisfied with the nectar of contentment are not available to the greedy who run here and there chasing wealth.
slokas.when_to_use: When the endless chase for more money is robbing you of peace. Contentment is the greatest wealth.

slokas.sanskrit: अनालोच्य व्ययं कर्ता ह्यनाथश्चलचित्तकः। सर्वत्राविमृशन्कर्ता स शीघ्रं विनश्यति॥
slokas.transliteration: anālocya vyayaṁ kartā hy anāthaś cala-cittakaḥ | sarvatrāvimṛśan kartā sa śīghraṁ vinaśyati ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who spends without thinking, who has no support system, whose mind is fickle, and who acts without reflection — such a person perishes quickly.
slokas.when_to_use: When spending impulsively or making rash decisions. Think before you spend and act with deliberation.
